title: Made to Praise
subtitle: The soul, created from nothing, learns why it exists: to praise its Maker without end

stillness: Let nothing of this passing life call you away.
Cleave to the good of God; reach toward what is eternal, and let the things of time fall behind.
gospel: "Let us make man," said God, "to our image and likeness."
Praise is not beautiful in the mouth of a sinner.
If I would justify myself, my own mouth shall condemn me.
behold: Consider, at the very beginning of your creation,
what height, what dignity your Maker bestowed on you.
See, then, the loftiness of your creation,
and weigh again the love you owe in return.
Attend diligently to what it means
that you were made to the image and likeness of God.
Know that the image is the more worthy:
a horse, an ox, the other creatures may bear some likeness to a man,
but the image of a man no creature bears, except another man.
Consider how you are made to His image:
God forever remembers Himself, understands Himself, and loves Himself.
And you, if untiringly you remember God, understand God, and love God,
you will be to His image, for you strive to do what God forever does.
consider: From nothing, by His sole and unbought goodness, you were created,
and made to the likeness of God, that loving Him you might praise Him without end.
Why else would God lift you to so glorious a privilege of being,
unless He willed you to be bent, unceasingly, upon His praise?
For His praise gives righteousness here, and blessedness hereafter.
But if you praise, praise with your whole heart; in loving, praise.
He praises, yet not with his whole heart, whom prosperity coaxes to bless God but adversity holds back from the gift of blessing.
He praises, yet does not love, who in praising God seeks anything other than God Himself.
Let no care, no thought, no anxiety of mind stand empty of the praise of God.
Let no good fortune of this life call you back from His praise; let no adversity silence you.
Seek nothing from Him but Himself: He the end of your desire, He the reward of your labor, He the solace of this shadowed life, He the possession of that blessed life.
prayer: I know, and truly I confess,
that it would better befit a wretched sinner to lie prostrate before You,
and with weeping and groaning to beg pardon for his sins,
than to praise You with a polluted mouth.
Yet, trusting in Your most tender goodness and inborn sweetness,
I long to praise You from my inmost heart,
beseeching the depths of Your mercies
that You despise me not, a foul worm, a dead dog, a rotting corpse,
For if the Powers of heaven cannot worthily praise You,
how much less can frail man, rottenness and a worm?
Yet not for this must praise be left off;
for there is no creature that does not praise its Maker, or at least leave Him worthy of praise.
Shall I then not praise You, because I know myself unclean?
Do not even the worms and frogs, bred unclean from corruption, desire to praise You their Creator, as far as they know and are able?
Would that every sweet instrument of music, the harmonies of heaven and the melodies of earth, might rise up from the heart and sound forth praises worthy of You.

latinJewel.latin: Ad laudandum igitur Conditorem tuum creata es, ut ejus laudibus vacans, et hic justitiæ merito in illum semper proficias, et in futuro beate vivas.
latinJewel.english: You were created, then, to praise your Maker, so that, giving yourself to His praises, you may here ever advance toward Him by the merit of righteousness, and hereafter live in blessedness.
